title: Masafee LoRA: Single-GPU LoRA Fine-tuning of an Original Character on Stable Diffusion
description:
Empirical study of LoRA fine-tuning a Stable Diffusion model to reproduce an original character ("Masafee", a red panda) on a single consumer GPU (NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 12GB). The project compares six training conditions across base models, learning rates, and LoRA ranks, and runs an epoch-extension ablation up to 60 epochs.

Key findings: (1) output quality is most strongly determined by the base model choice; (2) training plateaus around epoch 30 before drifting to overfitting; (3) output quality is bounded by the training image count.

Paper (Japanese / English, two-column PDF), training scripts, the best LoRA weights, and full comparison grids are included. The trained LoRA weights are also distributed on Hugging Face.
